buggy_code:
import vectorbt as vbt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)['close']

# Compute signals using only past data
fast_ma = prices.rolling(20).mean().shift(1)
slow_ma = prices.rolling(50).mean().shift(1)

entries = fast_ma > slow_ma
exits = fast_ma < slow_ma

# Run backtest with realistic fees
pf = vbt.Portfolio.from_signals(
    prices,
    entries,
    exits,
    fees=0.001,
    slippage=0.0005,
    freq='1D'
)

print(pf.stats())
clean_code:
import vectorbt as vbt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)['close']

fast_ma = prices.rolling(20).mean().shift(1)
slow_ma = prices.rolling(50).mean().shift(1)

entries = fast_ma > slow_ma
exits = fast_ma < slow_ma

pf = vbt.Portfolio.from_signals(
    prices,
    entries,
    exits,
    fees=0.001,
    slippage=0.0005,
    freq='1D'
)

print(pf.stats())
description: Clean example — vectorbt backtest with shifted signals and realistic fees and slippage.
explanation: Both moving averages are shifted by 1 before generating signals. Realistic fees (10 bps) and slippage (5 bps) are included. No lookahead bias.
